nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo Database — 炸排骨 Deep Fried Special Marinated Pork Chop (cr_id: G00010057)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All core per_100g values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 炸排骨 Deep Fried Special Marinated Pork Chop (cr_id: G00010057), which is an approved primary source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat (3.2g/100g) is supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — pork, fresh, loin, center rib (chops), bone-in, cooked, pan-fried (FDC ID 167903) as the closest approved-source equivalent, since HPB did not report saturated fat for this entry; this substitution is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per HPB Singapore Foodo (G00010057)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Carbohydrate content is 0.0g/100g as reported by HPB Singapore Foodo (G00010057). Glycemic index is not applicable for this food as it contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI testing is performed on foods with zero carbohydrate content; this is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database methodology, which does not assign GI values to foods with negligible available carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition (GL = GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100; with carbohydrate_g = 0, GL = 0).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050mg/100g — far exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; a single 150g serving delivers approximately 1575mg sodium, representing over 68% of the 2300mg daily sodium limit, and the soy-sauce-based marinade is the primary contributor.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0g/100g) supports glycaemic stability, but high fat from deep-frying may impair long-term insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the recorded carbohydrate content is 0g/100g and the food does not acutely raise blood glucose, frequent consumption of deep-fried foods is associated with worsened insulin resistance and should be limited to occasional intake for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050mg/100g), estimated saturated fat (~3.2g/100g), cholesterol (89mg/100g), and deep-frying process elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high in sodium and saturated fat from both the pork and frying oil, and the deep-frying process generates oxidised lipids; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and choose grilled or steamed lean pork preparations instead.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); pork contains arachidonic acid; sodium exceeds RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-fried preparation is a hard disqualifier for rheumatoid arthritis management due to AGE formation and pro-inflammatory lipid oxidation; individuals with RA should choose grilled or steamed pork and avoid this preparation entirely.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Deep Fried Special Marinated Pork Chop (炸排骨, Zhá Páigǔ) is a popular Chinese-Singaporean dish consisting of a pork chop that has been marinated in a complex blend of soy sauce, garlic, five-spice powder, and other aromatics before being deep-fried to a golden, crispy exterior. The meat inside remains juicy and tender, while the crust delivers a satisfying crunch. This dish is commonly found in Singaporean hawker centres, Chinese restaurants, and zi char stalls, typically served as a main course at lunch or dinner alongside steamed rice and vegetables. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 171 kcal, 19.1g protein, 10.5g fat, and 0g carbohydrate as reported by the HPB Singapore Foodo database. Sodium content is notably high at 1050mg/100g due to the soy-sauce-based marinade, making this dish a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The deep-frying process adds saturated fat and generates oxidised lipids and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are pro-inflammatory. Despite negligible carbohydrate content, the high fat load from deep-frying may impair insulin sensitivity over time, making moderation advisable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for halal diets due to pork content, and contains soy as a common allergen from the marinade.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id: G00010057), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. The record is marked as reviewed with confidence 0.95 in the source system. Saturated fat is supplemented from USDA FDC SR Legacy as an approximate estimate since HPB did not report it for this entry.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010057); this is a protein-and-fat-dominant deep-fried pork dish with no starch or sugar component reported. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB — pure meat dish with no plant fibre. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g/100g); gi_category set to 'not_applicable'. Saturated_fat_g (3.2g/100g) is an approximate estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for pork loin chop, cooked, pan-fried (FDC ID 167903) as the closest approved-source equivalent, as HPB did not report saturated fat for this entry. Trans_fat_g (0.1g/100g) is an estimate based on typical deep-frying oil contribution as noted in the source health_suitability data. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, water content, and micronutrient data not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo for this entry, and no sufficiently close approved-source equivalent was available to supplement these values without introducing unacceptable estimation error. Potassium (47mg/100g), calcium (19mg/100g), magnesium (6mg/100g), iron (1.14mg/100g), and cholesterol (89mg/100g) are sourced directly from HPB. The dish is not halal due to pork content. Soy sauce in the marinade is a common allergen source.
